mnemonics.title: sigpending Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: SIGPENDING = SIGnal PENDING - It tells you which signals are waiting in the PENDING queue because they were BLOCKED. Remember: PENDING = Generated but not delivered.

mnemonics.title: Signal Set Functions
mnemonics.content:
EMPTY = Initialize to empty (no signals)
FILL = Fill with all signals
ADD = Add a signal to set
DELETE = Delete from set
MEMBER = Check if signal is member

Pattern: E-F-A-D-M for easy recall: Empty, Fill, Add, Delete, Member

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal PENDING - tells which signals are waiting to be delivered
acronyms.usage: Used to check which blocked signals have arrived

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal SET Type - data type representing a set of signals
acronyms.usage: Used as parameter to sigpending, sigprocmask, and other signal functions

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal PROCess MASK - manipulate the signal mask
acronyms.usage: Used to block or unblock signals

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal SUSPEND - atomically change mask and wait
acronyms.usage: Used for atomic signal mask change and waiting

visualTips.visualization: Think of signal delivery as a queue: Generated → [Signal Mask Check] → If blocked → PENDING queue → When unblocked → DELIVERED to handler. Draw a flowchart: Signal arrives → Is it blocked? → Yes: Add to pending set → No: Deliver immediately.

Imagine sigset_t as a row of light switches (one per signal type). sigemptyset turns all OFF, sigfillset turns all ON, sigaddset turns one ON, sigdelset turns one OFF. sigismember checks if a specific switch is ON.
Draw two overlapping circles: Circle 1 = Blocked Signals (from sigprocmask), Circle 2 = Pending Signals (from sigpending). A signal is pending ONLY if it's in both circles (blocked AND generated).

analogies.analogy: Like email notifications on silent phone: The signal (email) was generated (sent) but is pending (silenced/pending) because your phone is on silent (signal masked). When you turn off silent (unblock), the notification delivers.

sigprocmask() is like setting up a bouncer list (who gets in), sigpending() is like checking who is waiting in line outside (pending). A person on the bouncer list (blocked signal) who arrives becomes pending in line.

Like a seat belt indicator in car - each position can be buckled (signal in set) or empty (signal not in set). sigemptyset = all empty, sigfillset = all buckled, sigaddset = buckle one, sigismember = check if occupied.
